page: Google Cloud Platform c4-highcpu-96 is a compute-optimized virtual machine powered by an Intel Xeon 8581C processor. It features 96 vCPUs mapped to 48 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled, operating at a base clock speed of 2.3 GHz. The system is equipped with 192 GB of RAM, providing a resource density of 2.0 GB per vCPU. It does not include local storage or integrated GPUs. The processor architecture is x86_64 and includes advanced instruction sets such as Advanced Vector Extensions (AVX-512) and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) for tile and integer operations.

Benchmark results indicate strong multi-core processing capabilities, with top-tier performance in Passmark CPU tests, bzip3 decompression, and Geekbench multi-core evaluations. The server demonstrates high-throughput capabilities in static web serving and database operations. Memory bandwidth is robust, reaching high read and write speeds for uncached large blocks, though single-core performance and memory latency tests fall into the average range. The hardware excels at parallelized tasks, showing strong results in software compilation, ray tracing, and large-scale text processing.

Given its high compute density and lack of bundled local storage, this server is highly cost-efficient for compute-bound workloads that do not require massive memory footprints or local disk I/O. It is well-suited for high-performance computing, multi-threaded data processing, static web serving, and CPU-based machine learning inference, particularly for prompt processing of small to medium language models.
description: Google Cloud Platform c4-highcpu-96 is a compute-optimized server configured with 96 vCPUs and 192 GB of RAM, powered by an Intel Xeon 8581C processor. Operating on the x86_64 architecture with 48 physical cores, it features advanced instruction sets including AVX-512 and Intel AMX. The instance does not include local storage or GPUs. In benchmark testing, the server demonstrates top-tier multi-core performance, particularly in Passmark CPU tests, multi-threaded decompression, and static web serving throughput. It also shows strong capabilities in CPU-based LLM prompt processing. With a 2.0 GB per vCPU memory ratio, this server is highly efficient for parallelized, compute-heavy workloads. It is recommended for software compilation, high-throughput web serving, database operations, and CPU-bound data processing tasks.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform c4-highcpu-96 is a compute-optimized server featuring 96 vCPUs, 192 GB RAM, and an Intel Xeon 8581C processor, delivering top-tier multi-core performance for parallelized workloads.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform c4-highcpu-96 is a compute-optimized server with 96 vCPUs, 192 GB RAM, and an Intel Xeon processor for high-compute workloads.
tagline: A high-density compute instance featuring ninety-six virtual cores and advanced matrix extensions for parallelized processing and high-throughput workloads.

Advanced instruction support including AVX-512 and Intel AMX
Top-tier multi-core performance in CPU benchmarks and decompression
Optimized for CPU-based LLM prompt processing and static web serving
